dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HRES 1027 IH: Supporting the designation of the “International Year of the Woman Farmer” to recognize and honor the critical role of women in agriculture.
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Ms. Pingree (for herself, Mrs. Hinson, Ms. Craig, Mr. Newhouse, Ms. Brown, Mr. Lucas, Mr. Gray, Mr. Bost, Ms. Tokuda, Mrs. Cammack, Mr. Davis of North Carolina, Mr. Mann, Ms. Budzinski, Mr. Rose, Mr. Bishop, Ms. Salinas, Ms. Kaptur, Mrs. Hayes, Mrs. McClain Delaney, and Mr. Costa) submitted the following resolution; which was referred to the Committee on Agriculture

form.official-title: Supporting the designation of the International Year of the Woman Farmer to recognize and honor the critical role of women in agriculture.

whereas.text: Whereas the United States proudly recognizes its agricultural heritage and acknowledges the vital role that women have played in advancing agriculture both domestically and globally;

Whereas women are essential to agriculture, serving as leaders on and off the farm, with more than 1,200,000 female agricultural producers in the United State comprising 36 percent of the agricultural producers of the United States;

Whereas, in addition to their leadership in farming and ranching operations, women in agriculture continue to make significant contributions across a wide range of sectors, including research and development, manufacturing, sales and distribution, agricultural education, agribusiness, and advocacy, benefitting communities in the United States and around the world;
Whereas 2026 has been designated as the International Year of the Woman Farmer; and
Whereas women professionals and leaders in agriculture in the United States should be recognized and celebrated for their contributions during this designated year: Now, therefore, be it

paragraph.text: supports the designation of the International Year of the Woman Farmer;
recognizes the critical role of women in agriculture; and

subparagraph.text: honor and recognize the contributions of women working in agriculture; and
celebrate the positive impact these women have on the food systems and agricultural workforce of the United States by encouraging and empowering women to—

clause.text: pursue careers in agriculture, a high-demand and essential field;
help feed a growing and hungry world.
